"Protective slats.<[silence:50ms]> Light control.<[silence:50ms]>
The word jalousy comes from French jalousie, meaning jealousy, as these slats were once used to spy on rivals unseen.<[silence:50ms]>
Jalousy windows use angled glass or wood slats to block sun and rain while allowing air and dim light through, like a one-way mirror for weather.<[silence:50ms]> In tropical homes, they keep interiors cool without darkness.<[silence:50ms]> Modern versions use adjustable aluminum slats for precision.<[silence:50ms]>
Three related subjects are louvered doors, Venetian blinds and passive cooling."
clean_story:
"Protective slats. Light control.
The word jalousy comes from French jalousie, meaning jealousy, as these slats were once used to spy on rivals unseen.
Jalousy windows use angled glass or wood slats to block sun and rain while allowing air and dim light through, like a one-way mirror for weather. In tropical homes, they keep interiors cool without darkness. Modern versions use adjustable aluminum slats for precision.
Three related subjects are louvered doors, Venetian blinds and passive cooling."
